heading: Death gratuity: miscellaneous provisions
label: 1480 Death gratuity: miscellaneous provisions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)1480(a)1480(b)1480(c)1480(d)38:1134(a).38:1133(e).38:1101(6)(B) (less 1st sentence, as applicable to death gratuity).38:1134(b)., §§ 102(6)(B) (less 1st sentence, as applicable to death gratuity) 303(e), 304(a), (b), , 869.Aug. 1, 1956, ch. 83770 Stat. 859In subsection (a), the words “was put to death” are substituted for the words “suffered death”. children.label: a
children.text: A payment may not be made under sections 1475–1477 of this title if the decedent was put to death as lawful punishment for a crime or a military offense, unless he was put to death by a hostile force with which the armed forces of the United States were engaged in armed conflict.

children.label: b
children.text: A payment may not be made under section 1476 unless the Secretary of Veterans Affairs determines that the decedent was discharged or released, as the case may be, under conditions other than dishonorable from the last period of the duty or training that he performed.
the hour on which the Reserve began to travel directly to or from the duty or training;
the hour at which he was scheduled to arrive for, or at which he ceased performing, that duty or training;
the manner in which the travel was performed; and

children.label: d
children.text: Payments under sections 1475–1477 of this title shall be made from appropriations available for the payment of members of the armed force concerned.
the date that is three years after the individual reaches 21 years of age; or
the date that is six years after the date of the death with respect to which the claim is made.
